# Transcrição: Watch_this_if_you've_had_a_mid-life_crisis...

---

## [00:00 - 01:28]

People go through this thing they call it midlife crisis, right? I think a midlife crisis is when you know exactly what's going to happen every single day of the week, every single month of the year, every single year moving forward. When you know how everything is going to be, I feel like that's what a midlife crisis is, is when people just feel like, oh, what am I doing with my time? Everything is, there are no more surprises. There are no more chapters of adventure and exploration. That's why I often tell my friends, I think the most interesting people that I know in my network are people who are still working out what they want to do next. So I think that's really inspiring, you know, I think it's really inspiring when people give themselves the permission to reinvent themselves. And I've reinvented myself many times. Some of you don't know this about me, but I started very early on. I started as an electronics entrepreneur. I went to China to the Guangzhou Fair and I bought a container of electronics back to Australia and I sold them at the Sunday markets. I thought I was going to open up like a JB Hi-Fi. And then I left that world and I became a professional magician. And then for a period I thought, well, maybe I should become an accountant. You know, I lived a period of my life where I was a keynote speaker. I traveled all around the world speaking and I'm kind of leaving that world of keynote speaking now. I'm teaching communication skills a lot more. I want to become a YouTuber, right? I want to create more content and I just allowed myself to continually reinvent myself. And I think that's what keeps life interesting. Look, some people are going to find something that they want to do their entire lives and they do that. And that's great. That's great. You do you. If I only get to live this life once, I want to try many different things. I want to try doing lots of different things.
